message.content:
Explore a pasta .harness do projeto para entender:

1. Estrutura de arquivos existente
2. Formato dos arquivos de documentação em ./docs/
3. Padrões de nomenclatura
4. Conteúdo do Frontend_Style_Guide.md
5. Qualquer outro arquivo harness existente que possa servir de template

Leia todos os arquivos em .harness/docs/ para entender as convenções do projeto.

content.text: Vou explorar a pasta `.harness` do projeto para entender sua estrutura, documentação e padrões. Deixe-me começar com uma busca ampla.

content.text: Ótimo! Agora vou ler todos os arquivos na pasta `.harness` para entender a estrutura completa.

content.content:
1→# Frontend Style Guide — Sem Parar (Zustand-only + Hook Component Pattern)
     2→
     3→Welcome to the **Sem Parar Frontend Style Guide**.  
     4→This version consolidates: **Zustand as the only state manager**, **Hook Component pattern** for Screens and Atomic components, **services architecture (SOLID)**, and an **cache pattern**.
     5→
     6→> Sit down, have a cup of coffee and read this calmly. ☕
     7→
     8→---
     9→
    10→## :pushpin: Table of Contents
    11→- [Introduction](#introduction)
    12→- [Components & Screens — Hook Component Pattern](#components--screens--hook-component-pattern)
    13→- [Atomic Components — Hook Component Pattern](#atomic-components--hook-component-pattern)
    14→- [Project Organization](#project-organization)
    15→- [Services Architecture](#services-architecture)
    16→- [State Management (Zustand-only)](#state-management-zustand-only)
    17→- [Routes Naming](#routes-naming)
    18→- [Folder Naming Rules](#folder-naming-rules)
    19→- [Code Standards](#code-standards)
    20→- [Styles Patterns](#styles-patterns)
    21→- [Cache Pattern](#cache-pattern)
    22→- [Checklist](#checklist)
    23→
    24→---
    25→
    26→## Introduction
    27→
    28→The goal of this guide is to ensure **consistency, testability and scalability** across our frontend projects.
    29→
    30→Key decisions:
    31→- **Zustand** is the **exclusive** global/shared state manager.
    32→- UI follows the **Hook Component** pattern (logic hook + dumb view).
    33→- Domain logic lives in **`/services`** with **SOLID** layering.
    34→- Cache is **typed and reusable**.
    35→
    36→---
    37→
    38→## Components & Screens — Hook Component Pattern
    39→
    40→Each screen is split into a logic hook and a render-only component.
    41→
    42→```
    43→screens/Home/
    44→├── index.ts        # logic: export useHomeScreen()
    45→├── view.tsx        # render: export default HomeScreen
    46→└── Models/
    47→    └── index.d.ts    # types (Props, State, Handlers, Return)
    48→```
    49→
    50→**Models/index.d.ts**
    51→```ts
    52→export interface HomeParams {
    53→  welcome?: string;
    54→}
    55→export interface HomeState {
    56→  greeting: string;
    57→  isLoading: boolean;
    58→}
    59→export interface HomeHandlers {
    60→  onPressCTA: () => void;
    61→}
    62→export type UseHomeReturn = HomeState & HomeHandlers;
    63→```
    64→
    65→**index.ts (logic hook)**
    66→```ts
    67→import { useCallback, useMemo, useState, useEffect } from "react";
    68→import type { HomeParams, UseHomeReturn } from "./Models";
    69→import { useUserStore } from "@/services/user/store"; // Zustand slice example
    70→
    71→export function useHomeScreen(params?: HomeParams): UseHomeReturn {
    72→  const name = useUserStore((s) => s.name);
    73→  const [isLoading, setIsLoading] = useState(true);
    74→
    75→  useEffect(() => {
    76→    const t = setTimeout(() => setIsLoading(false), 300);
    77→    return () => clearTimeout(t);
    78→  }, []);
    79→
    80→  const greeting = useMemo(
    81→    () => params?.welcome ?? `Hello, ${name || "guest"}!`,
    82→    [params?.welcome, name]
    83→  );
    84→
    85→  const onPressCTA = useCallback(() => {
    86→    // Call services, analytics, navigation, etc.
    87→  }, []);
    88→
    89→  return { greeting, isLoading, onPressCTA };
    90→}
    91→```
    92→
    93→**view.tsx (render)**
    94→```tsx
    95→import React from "react";
    96→import { View } from "react-native";
    97→import { Text, Button } from "@semparar/design-system-app";
    98→import { useHomeScreen } from "./index";
    99→import type { HomeParams } from "./Models";
   100→
   101→type Props = HomeParams;
   102→
   103→export default function HomeScreen(props: Props) {
   104→  const { greeting, isLoading, onPressCTA } = useHomeScreen(props);
   105→
   106→  if (isLoading) {
   107→    return (
   108→      <View>
   109→        <Text>Loading...</Text>
   110→      </View>
   111→    );
   112→  }
   113→
   114→  return (
   115→    <View>
   116→      <Text>{greeting}</Text>
   117→      <Button onPress={onPressCTA} text="Continue" />
   118→    </View>
   119→  );
   120→}
   121→```
   122→
   123→---
   124→
   125→## Atomic Components — Hook Component Pattern
   126→
   127→```
   128→atomic/atoms/Button/
   129→├── index.ts        # logic: export useButton()
   130→├── view.tsx        # render: export default Button
   131→└── Models/
   132→    └── index.d.ts
   133→```
   134→
   135→**Models/index.d.ts**
   136→```ts
   137→export interface ButtonProps {
   138→  text: string;
   139→  disabled?: boolean;
   140→  onPress?: () => void;
   141→}
   142→export interface ButtonState {
   143→  pressed: boolean;
   144→}
   145→export interface ButtonHandlers {
   146→  handlePress: () => void;
   147→}
   148→export type UseButtonReturn = ButtonState & ButtonHandlers;
   149→```
   150→
   151→**index.ts (logic hook)**
   152→```ts
   153→import { useCallback, useState } from "react";
   154→import type { ButtonProps, UseButtonReturn } from "./Models";
   155→
   156→export function useButton(props: ButtonProps): UseButtonReturn {
   157→  const [pressed, setPressed] = useState(false);
   158→
   159→  const handlePress = useCallback(() => {
   160→    setPressed(true);
   161→    props.onPress?.();
   162→    setTimeout(() => setPressed(false), 150);
   163→  }, [props.onPress]);
   164→
   165→  return { pressed, handlePress };
   166→}
   167→```
   168→
   169→**view.tsx (render)**
   170→```tsx
   171→import React from "react";
   172→import { Pressable, View } from "react-native";
   173→import { Text } from "@semparar/design-system-app";
   174→import { useButton } from "./index";
   175→import type { ButtonProps } from "./Models";
   176→
   177→export default function Button(props: ButtonProps) {
   178→  const { pressed, handlePress } = useButton(props);
   179→
   180→  return (
   181→    <Pressable disabled={props.disabled} onPress={handlePress}>
   182→      <View>
   183→        <Text>{props.text}{pressed ? " •" : ""}</Text>
   184→      </View>
   185→    </Pressable>
   186→  );
   187→}
   188→```
   189→
   190→---
   191→
   192→## Project Organization
   193→
   194→```
   195→src/
   196→├── @types/            # Global TS types
   197→├── assets/            # Images, fonts, icons
   198→├── atomic/            # UI components (Hook Component pattern)
   199→│   ├── atoms/
   200→│   ├── molecules/
   201→│   └── organisms/
   202→├── modules/           # Microfrontends shared and native modules
   203→├── screens/           # Page-level (Hook Component pattern)
   204→├── services/          # Domain logic (SOLID layout)
   205→│   ├── api/           # HTTP layer (Axios/Fetch)
   206→│   ├── class/         # Class with logical requests
   207→│   ├── hooks/         # Specific service hooks
   208→│   ├── repository/    # Data abstraction & mapping
   209→│   ├── services/      # Business orchestration
   210→│   ├── storage/       # Storage orchestration with cache or not
   211→│   ├── store/         # Zustand slices (only)
   212→│   ├── models/        # Domain types & contracts
   213→│   ├── utils/         # Reusable service helps
   214→│   └── index.ts       # Public exports
   215→├── hooks/             # Cross-cutting React hooks
   216→├── routes/            # Navigation config
   217→├── settings/          # Env & global config (axios, etc.)
   218→└── utils/             # General-purpose helpers
   219→```
   220→
   221→> UI **never** calls API directly. All effects & side effects live in the **logic hooks** (which call `/services`).
   222→
   223→---
   224→
   225→## Services Architecture
   226→
   227→**Flow:** `UI → services → repository → api` and `UI → services → store (Zustand)`
   228→
   229→- **api/**: request clients; no domain logic.
   230→- **class/**: class with logical requests.
   231→- **hooks/**: specific service hooks.
   232→- **repository/**: maps transport to domain, aggregates sources.
   233→- **services/**: orchestration and business rules.
   234→- **storage/**: storage orchestration with cache or not.
   235→- **store/**: Zustand slices (single source of app state).
   236→- **models/**: types, DTOs, contracts.
   237→- **utils/**: reusable service helpers.
   238→
   239→Principles:
   240→- Single responsibility per folder/file.
   241→- No circular dependencies.
   242→- Public APIs exported via `services/index.ts` per domain.
   243→
   244→---
   245→
   246→## State Management (Zustand-only)
   247→
   248→- All global/shared state is **Zustand**.
   249→- Slices live under `/services/<domain>/store/`.
   250→- Use `persist` (with `createJSONStorage`) only when needed.
   251→- Keep setters pure; side effects go in hooks or services.
   252→
   253→**Slice example**
   254→```ts
   255→import type { StateCreator } from "zustand";
   256→import type { AuthActions, AuthState } from "../models/store";
   257→
   258→export type AuthSlice = {
   259→    auth: AuthState;
   260→    authActions: AuthActions
   261→};
   262→
   263→const initialAuth = {
   264→    user: null,
   265→    loading: false,
   266→    error: undefined,
   267→}
   268→
   269→export const createAuthSlice: StateCreator<AuthSlice, [], [], AuthSlice> = (set) => ({
   270→    auth: initialAuth,
   271→    authActions: {
   272→        setLoading: (v) => set((s) => ({ auth: { ...s.auth, isLoading: v } })),
   273→        setUser: (v) => set((s) => ({ auth: { ...s.auth, user: v } })),
   274→        setError: (v) => set((s) => ({ auth: { ...s.auth, error: v } })),
   275→        reset: () => set({ auth: initialAuth }),
   276→    }
   277→});
   278→```
   279→
   280→**Root Store**
   281→
   282→- Applies to ALL Sem Parar frontend apps — including SuperApp.
   283→
   284→```ts
   285→import { create } from "zustand";
   286→import { devtools, persist, createJSONStorage } from "zustand/middleware";
   287→import { immer } from "zustand/middleware/immer";
   288→
   289→import { createUserSlice, type UserSlice } from "@semparar/mf-app-user/src/newArch/user";
   290→import { createAuthSlice, type AuthSlice } from "@semparar/mf-app-yourmf";
   291→
   292→import storage from "~/redux/store/storage";
   293→
   294→/** Add your slice Type */
   295→export type RootStore = UserSlice & AuthSlice;
   296→
   297→export const useRootStore = create<RootStore>()(
   298→  devtools(
   299→    persist(
   300→      immer((...a) => ({
   301→        ...createUserSlice(...a),
   302→        ...createAuthSlice(...a), // Add your slice here
   303→      })),
   304→      {
   305→        name: "root-storage",
   306→        storage: createJSONStorage(() => storage),
   307→        partialize: (s) => ({
   308→          user: s.user,
   309→          auth: s.auth, // Add your store data
   310→        }),
   311→        merge: (persisted, current) => ({ ...current, ...(persisted as Partial<RootStore>) }),
   312→      }
   313→    ),
   314→    { name: "RootStore" }
   315→  )
   316→);
   317→```
   318→
   319→- For Use on Class.
   320→
   321→```ts
   322→class Auth {
   323→  private readonly setAuth;
   324→
   325→  constructor () {
   326→    this.setUser = useRootStore.getState().authActions.setUser;
   327→  }
   328→
   329→  function doSomething(data) {
   330→    this.setUser({ ...data });
   331→  }
   332→}
   333→```
   334→
   335→- For Use on Component/Screen.
   336→- UI never imports the store directly — uses selectors
   337→
   338→```ts
   339→const loading = useRootStore((s) => s.user.isLoading);
   340→const token = useRootStore((s) => s.auth.token);
   341→```
   342→
   343→---
   344→
   345→## Routes Naming
   346→
   347→Standardize as **`Acronym + Route`**:
   348→- ✅ `SPPayment` (Sem Parar Payment)
   349→- ✅ `FTHPayment` (Fintech Payment)
   350→
   351→---
   352→
   353→## Folder Naming Rules
   354→
   355→| Folder                              | Convention   | Examples                    |
   356→|-------------------------------------|--------------|-----------------------------|
   357→| `atomic`, `screens`, `routes`       | `PascalCase` | `HomeScreen`, `CarDetails`  |
   358→| `assets`, `hooks`, `services`, etc. | `camelCase`  | `useAuth`, `authService`    |
   359→
   360→---
   361→
   362→## Code Standards
   363→
   364→**Language**: English only.  
   365→**Naming**: `camelCase` (vars/functions), `PascalCase` (components/classes), `UPPER_SNAKE_CASE` (constants).
   366→
   367→**Explanatory variables**
   368→```js
   369→const onlyNumbersRegex = /^\d+$/;
   370→const getNumberValidation = (message) => (value) => !onlyNumbersRegex.test(value) && message;
   371→const isNumber = getNumberValidation("error message");
   372→isNumber(1);
   373→```
   374→
   375→**Semantic constants**
   376→```js
   377→const DAY_IN_MILLISECONDS = 86400000;
   378→setTimeout(doSomething, DAY_IN_MILLISECONDS);
   379→```
   380→
   381→**Library workarounds with link**
   382→```js
   383→// Refer to issue https://github.com/ngokevin/react-file-reader-input/issues/11#issuecomment-363484861
   384→// eslint-disable-next-line no-param-reassign
   385→event.target.value = "";
   386→```
   387→
   388→**JSDoc**
   389→```js
   390→/**
   391→ * Truncates an event name to the max length.
   392→ * @param {*} eventName
   393→ * @param {*} maxLength
   394→ */
   395→```
   396→
   397→**Hooks: multi-line deps & IIFE**
   398→```ts
   399→useEffect(() => {
   400→  (async () => {
   401→    await CustomMath.randomSum();
   402→    setSum(42);
   403→  })();
   404→}, [
   405→  setSum,
   406→]);
   407→```
   408→
   409→**Extract conditions**
   410→```ts
   411→const hasSelectedOffer = !!(selectedProduct || selectedLeasingOffer);
   412→if (!hasSelectedOffer && !individualOffer) return;
   413→```
   414→
   415→**Optional chaining**
   416→```ts
   417→const answers = formikForm?.values?.answers;
   418→```
   419→
   420→**JSX indentation**
   421→```tsx
   422→{
   423→  !isEnd && (
   424→    <Button text="CTA" onPress={handleNavigate} />
   425→  )
   426→}
   427→```
   428→
   429→**Early returns**
   430→```ts
   431→if (!data) return null;
   432→return <View />;
   433→```
   434→
   435→**Nullish coalescing**
   436→```ts
   437→return data ?? 0;
   438→```
   439→---
   440→
   441→## Styles Patterns
   442→
   443→- Do **not** use `StyleSheet` or `styled-components`.
   444→- Use **@semparar/design-system-app** for components, tokens, and theming.
   445→
   446→---
   447→
   448→## Cache Pattern
   449→
   450→Keep cache typed, serializable, and domain.
   451→
   452→```ts
   453→import storage from "~/redux/store/storage";
   454→
   455→export interface CachedData<T> {
   456→  timestamp: number;
   457→  data: T;
   458→}
   459→
   460→export interface StorageContract<T> {
   461→  get(): Promise<CachedData<T> | null>;
   462→  save(data: T): Promise<void>;
   463→  remove(): Promise<void>;
   464→}
   465→
   466→export class GenericStorage<T> implements StorageContract<T> {
   467→  constructor(private readonly key: string) {}
   468→
   469→  async get(): Promise<CachedData<T> | null> {
   470→    const json = await storage.getItem(this.key);
   471→    if (!json) return null;
   472→    return JSON.parse(json) as CachedData<T>;
   473→  }
   474→
   475→  async save(data: T): Promise<void> {
   476→    const payload: CachedData<T> = { timestamp: Date.now(), data };
   477→    await storage.setItem(this.key, JSON.stringify(payload));
   478→  }
   479→
   480→  async remove(): Promise<void> {
   481→    await storage.removeItem(this.key);
   482→  }
   483→}
   484→```
   485→
   486→**Guidelines**
   487→- One storage class per module/feature.
   488→- Types/interfaces live in `Models/` where appropriate.
   489→- Use `timestamp` for freshness/expiry strategies.
   490→- Never persist secrets/PII outside secure storage.
   491→- Keep payloads small and JSON-serializable.
   492→
   493→---
   494→
   495→## Checklist
   496→
   497→- [ ] Screens/Components follow **Hook Component pattern** (`index.ts` hook + `view.tsx` render)
   498→- [ ] Types live in **Models/**
   499→- [ ] Only **Zustand** for global/shared state under **/services/<domain>/store/**
   500→- [ ] UI never calls API directly (go through **/services**)
   501→- [ ] Code standards and styles patterns applied
   502→- [ ] Cache implemented via **GenericStorage** (when needed)
   503→- [ ] Routes and folder naming conventions followed
   504→
   505→---
   506→
   507→Happy shipping! 🚀

content.content:

1→import type { StateCreator } from 'zustand';
     2→import type { AdditionalCardActions, AdditionalCardState } from '../models';
     3→
     4→export type AdditionalCardSlice = {
     5→  additionalCard: AdditionalCardState;
     6→  additionalCardActions: AdditionalCardActions;
     7→};
     8→
     9→const initialAdditionalCard: AdditionalCardState = {
    10→  name: '',
    11→  isLoadingName: false,
    12→  isErrorName: false,
    13→  errorNameMessage: '',
    14→  birthDate: '',
    15→  limit: '',
    16→  isLoading: false,
    17→  isError: false,
    18→  errorMessage: '',
    19→};
    20→
    21→export const createAdditionalCardSlice: StateCreator<
    22→  AdditionalCardSlice,
    23→  [],
    24→  [],
    25→  AdditionalCardSlice
    26→> = (set) => ({
    27→  additionalCard: initialAdditionalCard,
    28→
    29→  additionalCardActions: {
    30→    setName: (name) =>
    31→      set((state) => ({
    32→        additionalCard: {
    33→          ...state.additionalCard,
    34→          name,
    35→          isLoadingName: false,
    36→          isErrorName: false,
    37→          errorNameMessage: '',
    38→        },
    39→      })),
    40→
    41→    setLoadingName: (isLoading) =>
    42→      set((state) => ({
    43→        additionalCard: {
    44→          ...state.additionalCard,
    45→          isLoadingName: isLoading,
    46→          ...(isLoading && {
    47→            isErrorName: false,
    48→            errorNameMessage: '',
    49→          }),
    50→        },
    51→      })),
    52→
    53→    setErrorName: (error) =>
    54→      set((state) => ({
    55→        additionalCard: {
    56→          ...state.additionalCard,
    57→          isLoadingName: false,
    58→          isErrorName: true,
    59→          errorNameMessage: error,
    60→        },
    61→      })),
    62→
    63→    clearNameError: () =>
    64→      set((state) => ({
    65→        additionalCard: {
    66→          ...state.additionalCard,
    67→          isErrorName: false,
    68→          errorNameMessage: '',
    69→        },
    70→      })),
    71→
    72→    setBirthDate: (birthDate) =>
    73→      set((state) => ({
    74→        additionalCard: {
    75→          ...state.additionalCard,
    76→          birthDate,
    77→        },
    78→      })),
    79→
    80→    setLimit: (limit) =>
    81→      set((state) => ({
    82→        additionalCard: {
    83→          ...state.additionalCard,
    84→          limit,
    85→        },
    86→      })),
    87→
    88→    setLoading: (isLoading) =>
    89→      set((state) => ({
    90→        additionalCard: {
    91→          ...state.additionalCard,
    92→          isLoading,
    93→        },
    94→      })),
    95→
    96→    setError: (error) =>
    97→      set((state) => ({
    98→        additionalCard: {
    99→          ...state.additionalCard,
   100→          isLoading: false,
   101→          isError: true,
   102→          errorMessage: error,
   103→        },
   104→      })),
   105→
   106→    clearError: () =>
   107→      set((state) => ({
   108→        additionalCard: {
   109→          ...state.additionalCard,
   110→          isError: false,
   111→          errorMessage: '',
   112→        },
   113→      })),
   114→
   115→    reset: () =>
   116→      set({
   117→        additionalCard: initialAdditionalCard,
   118→      }),
   119→  },
   120→});
   121→

import type { AdditionalCardSlice } from '../store';

export type RootStore = AdditionalCardSlice;

content.text:
Perfeito! Agora tenho uma visão completa. Vou preparar um relatório detalhado sobre a pasta `.harness` do projeto.

## Relatório Completo: Exploração da Pasta `.harness` do Projeto

Realizei uma exploração minuciosa da pasta `.harness` e de toda a arquitetura do projeto para entender os padrões, convenções e estrutura. Aqui está o análise completa:

---

### 1. **Estrutura de Arquivos Existente**

A pasta `.harness` possui a seguinte organização:

```
.harness/
├── CLAUDE.md                                    # Instruções para Claude
├── docs/
│   ├── 0.info.md                              # Informações iniciais do projeto
│   └── 1.Frontend_Style_Guide.md               # Guia de estilos frontend (13.3 KB)
└── SPMAIS-33081-CASHBACK-MILHAS-WEBVIEW__TASK.pdf  # Especificação de task
```

**Localização completa:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-card-place/.harness/`

---

### 2. **Formato dos Arquivos de Documentação em `./docs/`**

Os arquivos seguem este padrão de nomenclatura e estrutura:

#### **Convenção de Nomenclatura:**
- Prefixo numérico (`0.`, `1.`, `2.`, etc.) para ordenação sequencial
- Nome descritivo em inglês e formato Markdown (`.md`)
- Organização lógica: informações gerais → guias de estilo → instruções específicas

#### **Arquivos presentes:**

**`0.info.md`** (572 bytes) - Arquivo Inicial
- Descreve contexto do projeto
- Informa sobre dependências (redux + sagas → Zustand)
- Orienta sobre PRs preliminares

**`1.Frontend_Style_Guide.md`** (13.3 KB) - Guia Completo
- Documentação estruturada com Markdown
- Inclui Table of Contents e seções bem definidas
- Exemplos de código em blocos formatados
- Padrões visuais com emojis e formatação clara

---

### 3. **Padrões de Nomenclatura Identificados**

#### **Pastas:**

| Tipo | Padrão | Exemplos |
|------|--------|----------|
| Componentes/Screens | `PascalCase` | `HomeScreen`, `FintechHome`, `InvoiceCashback` |
| Serviços/Módulos | `camelCase` | `additionalCard`, `authService`, `userService` |
| Utilitários | `camelCase` | `useAuth`, `apiClient`, `formatDate` |
| Assets | `camelCase` | `assets/icons`, `assets/images` |

#### **Arquivos:**

| Contexto | Padrão | Exemplos |
|----------|--------|----------|
| Componentes (lógica) | `index.ts` ou `index.tsx` | Exporta hooks de lógica |
| Componentes (view) | `view.tsx` | Renderização pura |
| Tipos | `Models/index.d.ts` | Contratos TypeScript |
| Zustand Store | `store/index.ts` | Estados globais |
| Serviços | `services/index.ts`, `class/index.ts` | Lógica de negócio |

---

### 4. **Conteúdo do Frontend_Style_Guide.md**

**Localização:** `/Users/anotonio.silva/dev/rnm-app-fintech-card-place/.harness/docs/1.Frontend_Style_Guide.md`

Este é um guia abrangente com os seguintes tópicos:

#### **Seções Principais:**

1. **Introduction** - Decisões arquiteturais-chave
2. **Components & Screens — Hook Component Pattern** - Padrão logic hook + view
3. **Atomic Components — Hook Component Pattern** - Átomos seguindo o mesmo padrão
4. **Project Organization** - Estrutura completa de diretórios
5. **Services Architecture** - Fluxo `UI → services → repository → api`
6. **State Management (Zustand-only)** - Zustand como único gerenciador global
7. **Routes Naming** - Padronização `Acronym + Route`
8. **Folder Naming Rules** - PascalCase vs camelCase
9. **Code Standards** - Inglês, casos de nomenclatura, padrões
10. **Styles Patterns** - Design System Sem Parar
11. **Cache Pattern** - GenericStorage com tipagem
12. **Checklist** - Validação antes de mergear

#### **Principais Descobertas:**

- **Padrão Hook Component:** Divide lógica (hook em `index.ts`) de renderização (`view.tsx`)
- **Zustand Obrigatório:** Único gerenciador de estado global (sem Redux para novos code)
- **Arquitetura em Camadas:**
  ```
  UI (Components/Screens)
    ↓
  Logic Hooks (useHomeScreen)
    ↓
  Services (regras de negócio)
    ↓
  Repository (mapeamento de dados)
    ↓
  API (cliente HTTP)
  ```
- **Tipos em Modelos:** Interfaces separadas para Props, State, Handlers, Return
- **Cache Tipado:** Implementação com `GenericStorage<T>` genérica
- **Language:** Código em **inglês** obrigatoriamente

---

### 5. **Arquivos Harness Existentes como Templates**

#### **`CLAUDE.md`** (54 bytes)
```markdown
# RULES:
- Follow rules and instructions in ./docs/*
```
Propósito: Instruir Claude (este bot) a seguir documentação em `./docs/`.

#### **`0.info.md`** (572 bytes) - Conteúdo:
- Projeto é um micro frontend do superapp (~/dev/appSemPararv3)
- TypeScript/Linter com limitações conhecidas
- PRs devem criar estrutura básica de domínios primeiro
- **Migração em andamento:** Redux + Sagas → Zustand

#### **`1.Frontend_Style_Guide.md`** - Template Completo
Este é o principal documento de referência. Contém exemplos práticos de:
- Estrutura de pastas
- Código de componentes (type-safe)
- Configuração de Store Zustand
- Padrões de cache
- Checklist de validação

---

### 6. **Convenções Detectadas no Projeto Real**

Analisando a implementação atual em `src/`:

#### **Estrutura de Diretórios (Confirmada):**
```
src/
├── @types/              # Tipos globais TypeScript
├── assets/              # Imagens, ícones, fontes
├── atomic/              # Componentes UI (Hook pattern)
│   ├── atoms/           # Componentes básicos
│   ├── molecules/       # Composições simples
│   └── organisms/       # Composições complexas
├── screens/             # Telas/Páginas (Hook pattern)
├── services/            # Lógica de domínio
│   ├── <domain>/
│   │   ├── api/         # Cliente HTTP
│   │   ├── class/       # Classes com requisições
│   │   ├── models/      # Tipos/DTOs
│   │   ├── hooks/       # Hooks específicos do serviço
│   │   ├── repository/  # Mapeamento de dados
│   │   ├── services/    # Orquestração
│   │   ├── store/       # Zustand slice
│   │   └── index.ts     # Exporta públicos
├── hooks/               # Hooks reutilizáveis
├── routes/              # Configuração de navegação
├── settings/            # Env e config global
└── utils/               # Helpers genéricos
```

#### **Exemplos Reais Encontrados:**

**Screen (Hook Pattern):**
- `/screens/Example/index.tsx` - Lógica com Redux (padrão antigo)
- `/screens/Example/view.tsx` - Renderização pura
- `/screens/Example/Models/index.ts` - Tipos (Props, PropsView)

**Serviço (Novo Padrão):**
- `/services/additionalCard/store/index.ts` - Zustand slice com ações
- `/services/additionalCard/api/index.ts` - Classe AdditionalCardApi
- `/services/additionalCard/models/store.d.ts` - Tipos para store

**Exemplos de Store Zustand:**
```typescript
// Slice pattern
export type AdditionalCardSlice = {
  additionalCard: AdditionalCardState;
  additionalCardActions: AdditionalCardActions;
};

export const createAdditionalCardSlice: StateCreator<...> = (set) => ({
  additionalCard: initialState,
  additionalCardActions: {
    setName: (name) => set((state) => ({ ... })),
    setLoading: (isLoading) => set((state) => ({ ... })),
    reset: () => set({ ... })
  }
});
```

---

### 7. **Princípios-Chave para Novas Tasks**

Com base na documentação:

1. **Usar Zustand** para estado global (não Redux)
2. **Hook Component Pattern** obrigatório para Screens e Atomic Components
3. **Tipos em Models/** - Separar interfaces de implementação
4. **Fluxo de Dados Unidirecional:**
   - UI chama hooks
   - Hooks chamam services
   - Services chamam API via repository
5. **Sem chamadas diretas de API** na UI
6. **Cache com GenericStorage<T>** quando necessário
7. **Código em inglês** (comentários, nomes, documentação)
8. **Routes:** Padrão `AcronymRoute` (ex: `SPPayment`, `FTHPayment`)

---

### 8. **Checklist para Novas Implementações**

Conforme descrito em `1.Frontend_Style_Guide.md`:

- [ ] Screens/Components seguem **Hook Component pattern** (`index.ts` + `view.tsx`)
- [ ] Tipos vivem em **Models/**
- [ ] Apenas **Zustand** para estado global sob **/services/<domain>/store/**
- [ ] UI nunca chama API diretamente (via **/services**)
- [ ] Padrões de código e estilos aplicados
- [ ] Cache via **GenericStorage** quando necessário
- [ ] Nomes de rotas e pastas seguem convenções
- [ ] TypeScript types completos e bem estruturados

---

### 10. **Resumo Executivo**

A pasta `.harness` serve como **base de conhecimento centralizada** para o projeto. Documenta:

1. ✅ **Padrões de código** (Hook Component, Zustand, tipos)
2. ✅ **Organização de diretórios** (estrutura modular clara)
3. ✅ **Convenções de nomenclatura** (PascalCase/camelCase conforme contexto)
4. ✅ **Arquitetura em camadas** (UI → services → repository → API)
5. ✅ **Cache tipado** (GenericStorage<T>)
6. ✅ **Checklist de qualidade** (antes de mergear PRs)

**Propósito da Pasta:** Garantir consistência, testabilidade e escalabilidade através de documentação clara e exemplos práticos.
